welcome_title: Welcome to the Trail Making Test
welcome_p1: The Trail Making Test measures visual attention, processing speed, and cognitive flexibility. It is widely used in neuropsychology to screen for executive dysfunction.
welcome_p2: The test has two parts. In Part A you connect the numbers 1 to 25 in order. In Part B you alternate between numbers and letters: 1-A-2-B-3-C and so on.
welcome_p3: Each part is preceded by a short practice round. Try to work as quickly and accurately as you can.
tutorial_step1_text: The screen shows circles labelled with numbers (Part A) or numbers and letters (Part B). Click them in the correct order.
tutorial_step2_text: Part A goes 1 → 2 → 3 → … → 25. Part B alternates: 1 → A → 2 → B → 3 → C → … → 13.
tutorial_step3_text: If you click the wrong circle it flashes red and is counted as an error. The timer does not stop — just click the correct next circle.
tutorial_step4_text: Your completion time and error count are both recorded. Part A has a 100-second limit and Part B a 300-second limit.
demo_intro: Watch how the sequence 1 → A → 2 → B is connected.
demo_step1: Circles appear on the board. Start with number 1.
demo_step2: Click 1 — a line is drawn from 1 towards the next target.
demo_step3: Next comes the letter A (number → letter).
demo_step5: Then a letter again: B. Keep alternating number, letter, number, letter.
demo_step6: That is the whole idea. Ready to practise?
instruction_practiceA_text1: Click the circles in ascending numerical order: 1, 2, 3, …
instruction_practiceA_text2: This is only a short practice round so you can get used to the task.
instruction_partA_text1: Connect the numbers 1 to 25 in order, as quickly and accurately as you can.
instruction_partA_text2: Your time and the number of errors are both recorded.
instruction_practiceB_text1: Now alternate between numbers and letters: 1 → A → 2 → B → 3 → C → 4 → D.
instruction_practiceB_text2: Remember: first a number, then a letter, then a number, then a letter.
instruction_partB_text1: Alternate numbers and letters in order: 1 → A → 2 → B → … → 13.
instruction_partB_text2: Work as quickly and accurately as you can.
countdown_instruction: Click the circles in the correct order.
segment_complete_next: Press Continue to move on to the next section.
all_segments_complete: You have finished both parts of the test. Press Continue to see your results.
feedback_wrong: Wrong circle — try the next one in the sequence.
results_interpretation: Part A reflects visuo-motor processing speed. Part B additionally taxes cognitive flexibility. A large B−A difference suggests a higher cost of set-switching.
settings_max_time_a_desc: Part A is aborted if this time is reached (standard: 100 s).
settings_max_time_b_desc: Part B is aborted if this time is reached (standard: 300 s).
settings_circle_size_desc: Diameter of the circles on the board.
footer_description: Trail Making Test — measures visual scanning, processing speed and cognitive flexibility.
